quote_of_day.text:
>>54174
I am not even exaggerating. I have actually lost sleep over aasi. I keep browsing ancestry forums in depressed state instead of sleeping. Every day i hope some new study confirms that aasi is fake, that OIT is true. Its all wishful thinking i know but i am genuinely depressed.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste tension moved into the board today, centered around a proposal to ban caste-baits. A divided community responded with a flurry of opinions, weighing in on the issue.

A scattered high-pressure ridge of technical complaints formed briefly, as one anon reported a fainting keyboard key. The issue resolved without incident.

Meanwhile, a cold front of regionalism blew in, bringing with it claims of south Indian inferiority to north Indians.

A nearby fog of criticism also rolled in, as an anon panned the reaction to incorrect online India maps, calling it pathetic.

A drizzle of concern about genetic heritage also appeared, with an anon inquiring about an individual's heritage.

articles.title: Anon Proposes Caste-Bait Ban, Community Divided
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with a poll, asking if users want /b/ to be free of caste-baits. The original post included a link to a straw poll, but noted that VPNs were blocked from voting.

One respondent suggested that /b/ should remain random and intentionally shitty to encourage users to post on other boards, which should have stricter moderation.

Another user agreed, stating that region baits are also a problem and often used by people from Jharkhand who don't even bother to change their IP addresses. However, some users expressed skepticism about the proposal.

One anon argued that making such a change would be a slippery slope and could lead to the censorship of certain topics. Another user claimed that the current moderator has an agenda and bans users unfairly.

Some users suggested changing the moderator or enforcing existing rules more strictly. One user proposed banning "pasmandas" to make /b/ more usable.

Others discussed the possibility of implementing new rules or restrictions, such as requiring users to post a specific phrase alongside their comments. The discussion also touched on the topic of user behavior and moderation.

One user stated that they would personally assume the OP to be a certain individual if they implemented the proposed changes. Another user expressed frustration with the community, saying that they are tired of moderating and dealing with problematic users.

As the discussion continued, users shared their thoughts on the current state of the board and potential solutions. One user suggested focusing on deleting certain types of content and banning users who post it, rather than trying to implement new rules.

Another user mentioned that they may not be as active on the board in the future due to college commitments.

articles.title: Anon Complains of Fainting Keyboard Key
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread saying their keyboard's 'A' key was fainting. One anon responded that the issue was not with the keyboard but with people learning English from other non-native speakers, suggesting that even 'high-class' people could have strong regional accents.

Another anon joked that Muslims reusing truck tires from their puncture repair shops was the reason for keyboard issues. A respondent sarcastically asked if the OP was Hindu and suggested they get a keyboard from a Muslim shop if they were having issues.

The conversation quickly devolved into a series of insults and vulgar remarks. Anon started baiting with phrases like 'Maa to pasmande ki chudi aaj bhi aur kal bhi' and 'Ya allah gaza ke baccho ko parathe khila ed'.

Others responded in kind, with one anon saying 'PASMANDE KI AMMI CHODO' and another claiming 'TOTAL BANIYAPOO DEATH NEEDED AT THIS MOMENT!!!!' The discussion included various personal attacks and expletives, with one anon asking if someone's penis was supposed to be a certain color.

The conversation remained hostile and off-topic throughout.

articles.title: Anon Claims South Indians Inferior to North Indians
articles.body:
Anon sparked controversy with the statement, "Its useless and ridiculous to see them fighting. Southies just need to accept they are indeed inferior to northies. Thats it." A respondent countered with a reference to a previous post, saying, "I've posted my hands earlier.

And all of you 'aryans' shut up. This is not about muh dark skin." They also mentioned that "There is no steppe matrilineal dna. But steppe patrilineal dna only.

This indicates one of the 2 things: Many of your male ancestors were killed and then females ancestors raped; Your male ancestors accepted some type of cuckold style arrangement with" others.

Another anon chimed in, saying that any Indian group can claim to be Aryan in a cultural sense, and that "Aryan" doesn't have a fixed meaning.

They added, "If Aryan = Steppe then no group can claim it" and questioned the importance of claiming Aryan heritage.

A different poster brought up the topic of paternal lineage, stating that Aryans themselves followed the paternal lineage, and that it's "a mark that you are a rape baby" to claim a patrilineage.

The discussion also touched on the genetic makeup of different groups, with one anon mentioning that "about 60% of jaats have r1a, with others having L, J, H.

So are 40% of jaats non-Aryan according to your logic?" Another poster joked, "Kek, so you admit your ancestors were raped lmao." The conversation was marked by disagreements and sarcastic remarks, with one respondent calling another "Yadav retard" and another saying "Literal Aboos are more aryan than these high AASImaxx moolniwasi baniyas".

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Reaction to Wrong India Maps Online
articles.body:
Anon expressed frustration with people who react strongly to incorrect India maps online, calling it "pathetic to watch" and likening it to a "toddler crying" when their sibling gets more attention.

One respondent argued that Indians misunderstand soft power, describing it as a subtle export of media, not direct coercion. Another shared an anecdote about a presentation where a map from British data was used, leading to humiliation from professors.

A different anon noted that countries like North Korea have successfully used soft power through media like K-dramas.

articles.title: Anon Worries About Genetic Heritage
articles.body:
Anon expressed concern about their genetic heritage, specifically about the presence of "aasi" in their blood. They felt that marrying someone with a different genetic background would dilute their own heritage.

Another anon responded, suggesting that their ancestors did not worry about such things and that they should not either.

A third anon chimed in, stating that everyone's ancestors cared about preserving their genetics, which is why conquests often resulted in genocide and the impregnation of a few spared females.

The discussion touched on the idea that if one does not care about genetics, they might as well live a hedonistic lifestyle with no point in reproducing or rearing children.
